model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer and schedule are already using the short budget better. The next likely gain is a modest capacity increase: one more pooled conv stage improves receptive field and feature abstraction, while slightly wider channels and a larger head keep the model compact enough for the fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous depth increase paid off, which suggests the model is still capacity-limited under the fixed-step regime. This keeps the same successful training setup and only widens the network and head to improve representation quality without changing the optimization dynamics.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Widening has improved validation loss on consecutive steps, so the model still appears capacity-limited. This keeps the same successful optimization recipe and makes one more controlled increase in feature width and head size to test whether the fixed budget can support a larger compact CNN.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model has scaled up successfully, and the remaining gains are likely to come from better generalization rather than more raw capacity. Label smoothing is a simple low-risk regularization change that often improves CIFAR validation loss with AdamW and short-budget training without altering the rest of the successful setup.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing degraded validation loss, so the next useful move is to remove that regularization and restore the sharper objective before exploring other directions. This is a clean rollback of a failed idea while preserving the stronger architecture and optimization settings.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After several successful capacity increases, the current model may be slightly under-driven within the same 256-step budget. A modest LR increase is a simple way to test whether the larger network can reach a better point faster without disturbing the rest of the working setup.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 optimizer steps, a lower Adam beta2 can make second-moment estimates adapt faster and sometimes improves early training on CNNs. This keeps the successful architecture and LR while testing a short-horizon optimizer tweak that is cheap and easy to revert.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current setup is converging well, so a lower end-of-run learning rate may help the last portion of the 256-step budget settle into a better minimum. This keeps warmup and overall cosine shape unchanged while removing the 10% LR floor.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Removing the LR floor hurt validation loss, so the scheduler should be restored to the better-performing cosine-with-floor variant. This cleanly rolls back the failed schedule change while keeping the stronger LR and optimizer settings.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The model is now strong enough that the fixed 256-step budget may be sample-limited rather than optimization-limited. A moderate batch-size increase lets each verifier run cover more training examples while keeping the rest of the validated setup unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strong improvement from batch size 320 suggests the run is still sample-limited at a fixed 256 steps. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch size has increased substantially and kept improving validation loss, so the optimizer may now tolerate and benefit from a correspondingly larger step size. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Both larger batches and a higher LR improved the current regime, which suggests the optimizer is still benefiting from more aggressive progress per step. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher LR overshot and worsened validation loss, so the next step is to restore the better-performing 2.5e-3 setting. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The fixed-step budget continues to benefit from larger batches, which indicates the current run is still limited by examples seen rather than raw optimization stability. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last two batch-size increases both improved validation loss, so the fixed 256-step budget still appears sample-limited. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.75e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The batch size has grown significantly since the last failed 3e-3 trial, so the current 2.5e-3 learning rate may now be slightly conservative.
